title: Job I/O throughput pattern analysis
difficulty_justification: Requires aggregating I/O data across multiple jobs and identifying statistical patterns, not just a single lookup.
query_text: What is the aggregate I/O throughput pattern for my last 5 computational jobs? Identify any jobs with unusually high write traffic and recommend optimization strategies.

eval_criteria.gold_answer: Job 901001 (alice, proj_hpc_ml) shows anomalously high write traffic: 40 GB write vs 10 GB read, suggesting checkpoint-heavy workflow. Mean read across 5 jobs: 38 GB; mean write: 12 GB — job 901001 writes 3× the average. Recommendations: (1) use Lustre striping (-c 8) for checkpoint directories; (2) consider in-memory checkpointing with DMTCP to reduce write frequency; (3) use collective MPI-IO (MPI_File_write_all) if writing in parallel.

checkpoints.description: Agent retrieves I/O metrics for last N jobs.

checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer identifies job_id 901001 or equivalent as high-write outlier

checkpoints.description: Agent cites storage_user_guide for optimization strategies.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer references Lustre striping or collective I/O from docs
